data.title: Waymo Swerves Past Dickens and Zebras to Get Set for London Self-Driving Launch
data.description:
Alphabet (GOOGL)-owned self-driving firm Waymo is coming to the streets of London this Spring after learning how to cope with the capital's Dickensian alleyways and zebra crossings.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Pilot Service in April

The company said a pilot robotaxi service would be launched in April, but it hopes to operate a full fleet as soon as September. That's if, as promised by the U.K. government, regulations are changed to allow driverless taxis to operate in the city by then.

Waymo is currently mapping the streets of the capital, but the vehicles are being operated by a safety driver and are not available to hire.

That mapping includes getting used to London's busy, narrow streets and quirks. "We have been learning the nuances, such as the zebra crossings," said Ben Loewenstein, head of EU and UK policy and government affairs at Waymo.

Zebra crossings are black-and-white road markings in the U.K. where pedestrians have right of way to cross. Cars must slow down to allow passengers to do so, but it is a case of eye contact between driver and nervous crosser rather than the use of lights to ensure it ends safely. One of the most famous zebra crossings is on Abbey Road in London and was used for the cover of the Beatles album of the same name in 1969.

Rivals Ready to Launch

Loewenstein was speaking at an event at the London Transport Museum in Covent Garden today where Waymo unveiled one of the Jaguar cars it hopes to use in the capital.

Waymo's cars are electric Jaguar iPACE vehicles while rivals Lyft (LYFT) and Uber (UBER) -who are also keen to launch services once the regulations change – have both chosen to partner with Chinese operator Baidu (BIDU).

In London, Waymo will work with Moove, a fleet management firm that handles maintenance and charging for ride-hailing companies. The partnership will help manage Waymo's electric cars and local operations. Waymo's system uses radar and lidar sensors to guide vehicles on the road, and the company says it has logged over 100 million miles without human control.

Waymo's move marks its first expansion into Europe and its second international market after Tokyo. The company already runs commercial robotaxi services in U.S. cities including San Francisco, Los Angeles, and Austin.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: From Bitcoin to Balance Sheets: Analyst Names 3 Crypto Stocks to Buy — MSTR, COIN and HOOD
data.description:
It's easy to think fintech has simplified finance, and in many ways, it has. Digital platforms make transactions feel faster and trading more convenient in an increasingly online world. But much of that speed is only on the surface. Banks still take days to settle many transactions, and the back-end systems have barely changed. What has changed is how customers interact with the financial system, not how the system itself operates.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Cryptocurrency breaks from this model because these assets were built for a digital environment from the start, using infrastructure designed for direct and rapid settlement. That distinction has drawn Wall Street's attention, but regulatory uncertainty continues to weigh on the sector. In the US, investors are waiting for resolution on the CLARITY Act, which remains stalled in the Senate and could shape how crypto trading is regulated.

Against this backdrop, Cantor analyst Ramsey El-Assal has been taking a deep dive into the segment, and highlights its unique selling point. "From crypto's inception, 'decentralization' has been a primary rallying cry of a libertarian-oriented community of early adopters. Removing fee-taking-intermediaries from financial transactions and ensuring that crypto remains 'pristine collateral' that cannot be debased by authorities are oft-cited reasons for the technology's existence. Not only are these noble motivations, but they make a great deal of sense in the context of crypto being a store of value or an asset. The stunning success of Bitcoin—whose uncompromising design elevates decentralization at the expense of financial usefulness—is a case in point," El-Assal wrote.

El-Assal goes on to lay out where opportunities in the industry are likely to open up: "We believe the primary value accrual in the crypto/blockchain ecosystem is likely to shift away from private projects and decentralized protocols and towards new and existing public companies that find ways to reap the benefits of blockchain technology, while also remaining compliant and generally insync with mainstream financial services. This thought applies primarily to fintech product innovators but certainly includes capital markets innovators such as MSTR and its emulators."

These comments back up the Cantor analyst's stance, as he names three crypto stocks to buy in today's market. Let's give them a closer look and find out just what makes them stand out. And with some assistance from the TipRanks database, we can also gauge general Street sentiment toward these names.

Strategy (MSTR)

The first crypto company we'll look at here is Strategy. The company has been buying – and holding – bitcoin since 2020, and currently holds over 712,000 of the flagship crypto. At current trading prices, this asset base is valued at ~$62.5 billion – and makes Strategy the world's largest bitcoin treasury company.

Strategy's name embodies the company's outlook on the business world: the firm accumulates bitcoins, and promotes the use of them as a digital capital asset. As a publicly traded company, Strategy offers its investors a controlled exposure to the crypto markets, and a chance to gain from bitcoin without buying bitcoin. In addition, Strategy also offers customers an AI-powered business intelligence/enterprise analytics platform that is trusted by such large names as Pfizer, KFC, Visa, and Sony.

That was how the company got its start, but the key for Strategy is in the bitcoin treasury. Recent disclosures show that the company purchased $264.1 million worth of bitcoin from January 20 to January 25, acquisitions that followed even higher purchases in the previous week; in the 7 days ending on January 19, the company purchased $2.13 billion worth of the crypto.

For El-Assal, in laying out the Cantor view, the most important factor is Strategy's ability to maintain its bitcoin investments while offering investors access through varied types of exposure. He writes, "We believe MSTR's approach of engineering purpose-built investment vehicles tailored to distinct risk appetites and return objectives across equity, credit, yield, and hybrid strategies not only broadens the investor funnel but also lowers the company's effective cost of capital, enhances balance sheet resilience, and mitigates liquidity risk during potential black swan events. In our view, MSTR's capital framework represents a structural innovation in public-company finance, positioning the business as a unique, scalable vehicle for BTC exposure while continually unlocking new sources of demand across the global financial ecosystem."

Quantifying his stance, the analyst rates the stock as Overweight (Buy), with a $213 price target that indicates a potential 34% gain in the year ahead. (To watch El-Assal's track record, click here)

The 13 recent analyst reviews here split 11 to 2 in favor of Buy over Hold, for a Strong Buy consensus rating. The shares are priced at $158.45, and the $439.36 average price target implies a robust one-year upside of 177%. (See MSTR stock forecast)
data.author: Michael Marcus

data.title: SoFi Technologies (SOFI) Q4 Earnings on Deck: Options Market Braces for an 8.48% Swing
data.description:
Fintech company SoFi Technologies (SOFI) is scheduled to announce its results for the fourth quarter of 2025 on Friday, January 30, and the options market is expecting a volatile reaction. Based on options pricing, traders are expecting an 8.48% move in either direction in SoFi stock following its Q4 2025 earnings. This implied move is higher than the stock's average post-earnings move (in absolute terms) of 5.73% over the past four quarters.

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 1/29/26 – U.S. Stock Futures Climb as Big Tech Earnings Loom
data.description:
U.S. stock futures edged higher early Thursday as traders reacted to Big Tech earnings and the Federal Reserve's decision to keep interest rates unchanged. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were up 0.36%, 0.25%, and 0.09%, respectively, at 3:47 a.m. EST on January 29.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Wednesday's regular trading, the three major averages closed mixed, with the S&P 500 briefly crossing the 7,000 mark before ending the day down 0.01%, following the Fed's interest rate decision. Meanwhile, the DJIA rose 0.02%, and the Nasdaq Composite edged up 0.17%, boosted by strong performances from Nvidia (NVDA) and Micron Technology (MU).

After the market closed, tech giant Microsoft (MSFT) stock dropped 6.1% due to slower cloud growth in the fiscal second quarter and softer margin guidance for the current quarter. In contrast, Meta Platforms (META) shares popped 7% on issuing a stronger-than-expected sales outlook for the first quarter and increased capex. Tesla (TSLA) stock rose 2.2% after beating Q4FY25 sales and earnings expectations despite reporting its first annual sales decline.

On the economic front, traders will watch data on weekly jobless claims, GDP growth, factory orders, and balance of trade.

Turning to earnings season, major companies reporting today include Apple (AAPL), Lockheed Martin (LMT), Mastercard (MA), Caterpillar (CAT), and Comcast (CMCSA).

Notably, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up, floating near 4.26%. WTI crude oil futures were trending higher, hovering near $64.17 per barrel as of the last check. Additionally, the Gold Spot U.S. dollar price increased to nearly $5,532 per ounce on Thursday, surpassing the $5,500 mark for the first time amid a flight to safety as the U.S. dollar weakened.

Elsewhere, European indexes opened mixed today as traders digested corporate earnings reports.

Asia-Pacific Markets Trade Higher

Asia-Pacific markets traded mostly higher on January 29, after the U.S. Fed's decision to hold rates steady and gold's continued rally.

Hong Kong's Hang Seng Index gained 0.51%. In China, the Shanghai Composite rose 0.16%, while the Shenzhen Component lost 0.37%. Meanwhile, Japan's Nikkei added 0.03%, and the Topix rose 0.28%.

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: META Stock Cheaper than GOOGL by 'Historical Norms,' Jefferies Says after Q4
data.description:
Meta Platforms (META) stock is up over 7% after it delivered a solid Q4 beat. Attention is turning to whether META's current valuation leaves room for upside. Jefferies weighed in after the report, with five-star analyst Brent Thill noting that META trades at roughly 21x forward earnings, compared with more than 28x for Alphabet (GOOGL). He stated that the gap is much wider than "historical norms," suggesting the stock is priced at an unusually large discount.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, Meta's earnings came in at $8.88 per share on $59.89 billion in revenue, beating Wall Street's expectations of $8.23 per share and $58.59 billion.

Jefferies Shrugs off Meta's High Capex    

Meta expects capital expenditures of up to $135 billion, nearly double last year's level, underscoring how aggressively the company plans to invest in AI. Wall Street estimates suggest that kind of jump in capex could cut operating margins by almost 5% this year.

Even so, Thill argues that the worst of the margin pressure is already reflected in META's stock price after its recent pullback. Meanwhile, even as Meta plans massive capital spending, it has scaled back spending on its metaverse division. Thill called that move a "positive sign," saying it shows the company is keeping a closer eye on costs.

Additionally, Thill said Meta's investments—especially in AI talent—are starting to pay off. To be precise, he noted that new text and image AI models expected in the first half of 2026 could shift the narrative around Meta's AI strategy, particularly after the Llama 4 model failed to impress.

Thill Is Bullish on META Stock

Thill reiterated a Buy rating on META stock with a $910 price target, implying more than 35% upside from current levels. He added that much of the near-term pressure on the business appears to be already priced into the stock.

Overall, Thill said Meta's revenue momentum and ongoing efficiency improvements should help offset higher operating costs, making the stock attractive to own into 2026.

What Is the Target Price for META?

On TipRanks, META stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 43 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 38 Buys and five Holds assigned in the last three months. The average META price target of $830.05, which implies 24% upside to the current levels.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) CEO Dumps $22M in Stock Post Q4 Results. Should Investors Worry?
data.description:
Johnson & Johnson (JNJ) CEO Joaquin Duato has sold a major block of company stock just days after the healthcare giant reported mixed fourth-quarter results. According to the SEC filing, Duato sold about $22 million worth of JNJ shares on January 26. The sale involved about 120,000 shares executed at prices ranging roughly between $180 and $185. While insider transactions of this size often draw attention, given the company's stable performance, the move does not signal a shifting sentiment.

Despite the sale, Duato holds a substantial stake in the company, signaling his ongoing long-term alignment with shareholders. He still directly owns over 275,967 shares and indirectly controls 110,486 shares.

data.title: SAP SE (SAP) Stock Falls 15% as Cloud Outlook Raises Concerns
data.description:
German software company SAP SE (SAP) shares fell nearly 15% today in pre-market trading (at the time of writing), after the company reported weaker growth in its cloud contract backlog for the fourth quarter.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The drop marked SAP's largest one-day decline since October 2020, when the stock fell 22% after weak quarterly results. At the same time, the stock moved toward its lowest closing level since mid-2024.

SAP said its current cloud backlog rose 16% in the fourth quarter to €21.1 billion, or about $25.3 billion. However, this figure came in below market expectations. UBS analysts noted that investors had been looking for growth closer to 26%, making the update a clear disappointment.

The company explained that some large deals pushed revenue further into future years. SAP said these contracts, along with required cancellation terms, reduced reported backlog growth by about one percentage point.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: SoFi Technologies' Earnings Are Coming – Watch Out for These KPIs
data.description:
SoFi Technologies (SOFI) will release its fourth-quarter fiscal 2025 results before the market opens on Friday, January 30. Investors should track SoFi's key performance indicators (KPIs) as they reveal past results and shape future expectations.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

A KPI is a quantifiable metric, such as revenue growth, profit margins, or daily active users, that reveals trends, efficiency, and potential issues like market shifts or resource inefficiencies.

SOFI's Prime KPI: Members

SoFi Technologies is a global fintech platform that offers digital financial services to help users borrow, save, spend, invest, and protect their money. Some of SoFi's KPIs include Members, Loan Origination Type, and Revenue by Segment.

However, SoFi's prime KPI is member growth. It directly fuels product usage and enables revenue scaling through cross-selling and higher lifetime value from engaged users. Tracking total members signals user growth and market penetration.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: 'CNN Not for Sale': Warner Bros. Stock (WBD) Holds despite Brushing Off Billionaire Barry Diller
data.description:
Warner Bros. Discovery's (WBD) shares managed to trade in the green early Thursday following reports that it did not give much consideration to billionaire media investor Barry Diller's interest in acquiring its popular cable network CNN.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

According to the Wall Street Journal, Diller, who is the chairman and senior executive of media and internet conglomerate IAC (IAC), expressed interest before Warner announced plans to split into two entities in June and before formally disclosing plans to sell its media holdings late last year.

Responding, the media company in a news report noted that "CNN is not for sale." The popular cable network argued that the company is central to Warner's profitable carriage deals with cable and satellite distributors.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: IonQ (IONQ) Adds Software and Secure Networks as It Builds a Full Quantum Platform
data.description:
IonQ Inc. (IONQ) continues to expand its quantum platform through two closely linked acquisitions focused on software and secure networking. Yesterday, the company announced an agreement to acquire Seed Innovations, a Colorado-based software and research firm, and completed its previously announced purchase of Skyloom Global.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

First, the Seed Innovations deal is expected to close on January 30, 2026, with financial terms not disclosed. Seed's team will join IonQ's Quantum Infrastructure unit, reporting to President Frank Backes. According to IonQ, Seed brings deep experience in AI, machine learning, cloud migration, and system automation. The goal is to help IonQ manage and scale complex quantum workloads for government and commercial customers.

Frank Backes said the acquisition expands IonQ's software depth as it builds an enterprise-grade, full-stack quantum platform. He added that Seed's skills in machine learning and automated architecture will help improve performance across IonQ's quantum systems.

Meanwhile, IonQ also completed its acquisition of Skyloom Global, a U.S.-based company focused on optical and secure communications. Skyloom adds free-space optical technology that supports quantum networking, quantum key distribution, and distributed quantum systems. Skyloom's leadership team will remain in place and continue working with existing government, aerospace, and defense customers.

IonQ Chairman and Chief Executive Officer Niccolo de Masi said Skyloom strengthens the company's ability to deliver secure connectivity and supports its long-term vision for distributed quantum networks.

Meanwhile, IONQ shares rose 0.68%, closing at $45.80.
data.author: Ran Melamed
